title: Sum a Decayed Array
prompt_md:
## Goal
A C-style array decays to a pointer when passed to a function, losing its length -- so the length must travel as a separate argument. Write a function that sums a decayed `int` array given a pointer and a length, and call it from `main`.

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 10).
- Line 2: `n` integers (each from -1000 to 1000).

## Output
One line: `sum: <total>`.

Do not use `std::array`, `std::vector`, or any container.

## Example
Input `4` then `10 20 30 40` produces `sum: 100`.

test_cases.label: Sample -- small array with positive values
Hidden -- mixed positive and negative values

title: Find the Maximum via Decayed Array
prompt_md:
## Goal
Demonstrate array decay at a function boundary. In `main`, `sizeof` on the C-style array reports the whole array's byte size; inside a function that receives the decayed pointer, `sizeof` reports only a pointer's size. Write a function that returns `sizeof` of its pointer parameter, and another that finds the maximum element of a decayed array given its length.

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 10).
- Line 2: `n` integers (each from -1000 to 1000).

## Output
```
array sizeof: <S1>
pointer sizeof: <S2>
max: <maximum>
```
where `<S1>` is `sizeof` of the local array in `main`, `<S2>` is `sizeof` of the decayed pointer parameter, and `<maximum>` is the largest of the `n` values. On Judge0, S1 is 40 and S2 is 8.

Do not use `std::array`, `std::vector`, or `<algorithm>`.
